calendar.matters:
Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Feb-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Compmany.
Dept. 514. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Nichole M. Rodich, CSR #11604. Telephone: 415-551-0616.

Michael Espinosa, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Cynthia Fitzgerald, as Wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in-Interest to Clinton Buffington, Deceased, and Bruce Buffington and Mary Croft, as Legal Heirs of Clinton Buffington, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Default was entered against defendant on April 18, 2012.
Statement of Damages was filed and served on April 18, 2012.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on January 8, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Cynthia Fitzgerald, as Wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in-Interest to Clinton Buffington, Deceased, and Bruce Buffington and Mary Croft, as Legal Heirs of Clinton Buffington, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendant Marin Engineering and Supply Company, as follows:

Economic Damages: $52,477.66.
Non-Economic Damages - $650,000.00.
Total - $702,477.66.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
